# @webeach/event-manager

> A lightweight JavaScript/TypeScript library that simplifies event listener management — subscribe, unsubscribe, and dispatch events with a clean chainable API and zero runtime dependencies.

## Problem it solves

Native `addEventListener` / `removeEventListener` requires keeping exact function references to remove listeners, leads to boilerplate when managing multiple events, and silently fails if options like `{ capture: true }` don't match on removal.

`@webeach/event-manager` tracks all registered handlers internally so you never hold references yourself. Remove one event, several, or all at once — in a single call.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @webeach/event-manager
# or
pnpm add @webeach/event-manager
# or
yarn add @webeach/event-manager
```

Browser via CDN (no build step):

```html
<script type="module">
  import { listen } from 'https://unpkg.com/@webeach/event-manager';
</script>
```

## Core API

### `listen(target?, handlers?)`

Creates an `EventManager` for the given target. Pass nothing or `null` to create a standalone custom event bus.

```js
import { listen } from '@webeach/event-manager';

listen(window);                          // Window events
listen(element);                         // DOM element events
listen(element, { click: handler });     // with initial handlers
listen();                                // custom event bus
```

### `.add(type, handler | handler[], options?)`

Registers one or more handlers. Returns `this` for chaining.

Options: `{ capture?: boolean, once?: boolean, passive?: boolean }`

```js
listen(button)
  .add('click', () => console.log('clicked'))
  .add('focus', () => console.log('focused'));

// Multiple handlers at once
listen(button).add('click', [handlerA, handlerB]);

// Options
listen(button).add('click', handler, { capture: true });
listen(button).add('click', handler, { once: true });
listen(button).add('scroll', handler, { passive: true });
```

### `.capture(type, handler | handler[])`

Shorthand for `.add(type, handler, { capture: true })`.

### `.once(type, handler | handler[])`

Shorthand for `.add(type, handler, { once: true })`. The handler fires once and is automatically removed.

### `.remove(type | type[])`

Removes handlers for specific event type(s). Only affects handlers registered through this instance.

```js
listener.remove('click');
listener.remove(['click', 'focus', 'keydown']);
```

### `.remove()`

Removes all handlers registered through this instance.

```js
listener.remove();
```

### `.trigger(type, detail?)`

Dispatches a `CustomEvent` with optional detail payload.

```js
listener.trigger('my-event');
listener.trigger('my-event', { value: 42 });
```

### `.trigger(event)`

Dispatches an existing `Event` object directly.

```js
listener.trigger(new CustomEvent('my-event'));
```

## Usage patterns

### Subscribe and unsubscribe

```js
const listener = listen(button).add('click', handler);

// Remove after 10 seconds
setTimeout(() => listener.remove('click'), 10000);
```

### Remove all at once

```js
const listener = listen(window)
  .add('focus', onFocus)
  .add('resize', onResize)
  .add('scroll', onScroll);

listener.remove(); // removes all three
```

### Initial handler map (shorthand)

```js
listen(button, {
  click: () => console.log('clicked'),
  focus: () => console.log('focused'),
  keydown: (e) => console.log(e.key),
});
```

### Custom event bus

```js
const bus = listen();

bus.add('greet', (e) => console.log(`Hello, ${e.detail.name}!`));

setTimeout(() => {
  bus.trigger('greet', { name: 'Alex' });
}, 1000);
```

### React useEffect cleanup

```jsx
useEffect(() => {
  const { remove } = listen(window, {
    scroll: () => setScrollY(window.scrollY),
    resize: () => setWidth(window.innerWidth),
  });
  return () => remove();
}, []);
```

### Tracking scroll position

```js
const listener = listen(window).add('scroll', () => {
  document.querySelector('.back-to-top').hidden = window.scrollY < 200;
});
```

## TypeScript

The library is fully typed. Event types are inferred automatically from the target.

```ts
// Window events — fully typed
listen(window).add('resize', (event) => {
  // event: UIEvent
});

// DOM element events — fully typed
listen(button).add('click', (event) => {
  // event: MouseEvent
});

// Custom event bus with typed events
interface AppEvents {
  'user:login': CustomEvent<{ userId: string }>;
  'cart:update': CustomEvent<{ count: number }>;
}

const bus = listen<EventTarget, AppEvents>();

bus.add('user:login', (e) => {
  console.log(e.detail.userId); // typed
});

bus.trigger('user:login', { userId: '42' });
```

## Key facts

- Zero runtime dependencies
- Works with `window`, any DOM element, and custom `EventTarget` instances
- Supports `capture`, `once`, and `passive` listener options
- All methods return `this` — fully chainable
- `remove([...])` validates all types atomically before removing any
- Destructuring-safe: `const { remove } = listen(el)` works correctly
- Bundle size: ~1 KB brotli-compressed

## Migration from native addEventListener

### Single event

```js
// Before
function handleClick(event) {
  console.log('clicked', event);
}
button.addEventListener('click', handleClick);
button.removeEventListener('click', handleClick);

// After
const listener = listen(button).add('click', (event) => {
  console.log('clicked', event);
});
listener.remove('click');
```

### Multiple events

```js
// Before
const handleClick = () => {};
const handleFocus = () => {};
const handleKeydown = () => {};

button.addEventListener('click', handleClick);
button.addEventListener('focus', handleFocus);
button.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeydown);

// Later...
button.removeEventListener('click', handleClick);
button.removeEventListener('focus', handleFocus);
button.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeydown);

// After
const listener = listen(button, {
  click: () => {},
  focus: () => {},
  keydown: () => {},
});

listener.remove();
```

### Capture phase

```js
// Before
element.addEventListener('click', handler, { capture: true });
element.removeEventListener('click', handler, { capture: true }); // must match!

// After
const listener = listen(element).capture('click', handler);
listener.remove('click'); // options are tracked internally, no need to repeat them
```

### Once

```js
// Before
function handleOnce(event) {
  doSomething(event);
  button.removeEventListener('click', handleOnce);
}
button.addEventListener('click', handleOnce);

// After
listen(button).once('click', (event) => doSomething(event));
```

## React patterns

### useEffect with single event

```jsx
// Before
useEffect(() => {
  const handler = () => setCount((c) => c + 1);
  window.addEventListener('click', handler);
  return () => window.removeEventListener('click', handler);
}, []);

// After
useEffect(() => {
  const { remove } = listen(window).add('click', () => setCount((c) => c + 1));
  return () => remove();
}, []);
```

### useEffect with multiple events

```jsx
// Before
useEffect(() => {
  const onScroll = () => setScrollY(window.scrollY);
  const onResize = () => setWidth(window.innerWidth);
  const onFocus = () => setFocused(true);
  const onBlur = () => setFocused(false);

  window.addEventListener('scroll', onScroll);
  window.addEventListener('resize', onResize);
  window.addEventListener('focus', onFocus);
  window.addEventListener('blur', onBlur);

  return () => {
    window.removeEventListener('scroll', onScroll);
    window.removeEventListener('resize', onResize);
    window.removeEventListener('focus', onFocus);
    window.removeEventListener('blur', onBlur);
  };
}, []);

// After
useEffect(() => {
  const { remove } = listen(window, {
    scroll: () => setScrollY(window.scrollY),
    resize: () => setWidth(window.innerWidth),
    focus: () => setFocused(true),
    blur: () => setFocused(false),
  });
  return () => remove();
}, []);
```

### Custom hook

```tsx
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { listen } from '@webeach/event-manager';

function useWindowEvent<K extends keyof WindowEventMap>(
  type: K,
  handler: (event: WindowEventMap[K]) => void,
) {
  useEffect(() => {
    const { remove } = listen(window).add(type, handler);
    return () => remove();
  }, [type, handler]);
}

// Usage
useWindowEvent('resize', () => console.log('resized'));
```

### Component with hover state

```tsx
// Before
const ref = useRef<HTMLButtonElement>(null);

useEffect(() => {
  const el = ref.current;
  if (!el) return;

  const onEnter = () => setText('Go to cart');
  const onLeave = () => setText('Shopping cart');

  el.addEventListener('mouseenter', onEnter);
  el.addEventListener('mouseleave', onLeave);

  return () => {
    el.removeEventListener('mouseenter', onEnter);
    el.removeEventListener('mouseleave', onLeave);
  };
}, []);

// After
const ref = useRef<HTMLButtonElement>(null);

useEffect(() => {
  if (!ref.current) return;
  const { remove } = listen(ref.current, {
    mouseenter: () => setText('Go to cart'),
    mouseleave: () => setText('Shopping cart'),
  });
  return () => remove();
}, []);
```

## Links

- [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@webeach/event-manager)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/webeach/event-manager/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)
